raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 2083 prohibits the use of ratepayer funds in criminal or civil settlement agreements where a utility has been deemed negligent in starting a fire.",
    "Currently, when an investor owned utility reaches a pre-trial settlement, the funds can be drawn from ratepayers, making Californians pay for a company’s negligence.",
    "In the last five years, California suffered an unprecedented number of wildfires due to utility negligence.",
    "AB 2070 requires utility companies to notify local fire districts before conducting high-risk 'hot work'.",
    "Notification ensures that local fire districts are prepared to respond quickly to potential fires, avoiding needless destruction.",
    "If PG&E had been convicted for starting the wildfires, all penalties would be paid back from profits.",
    "Because of a loophole, PG&E faced no meaningful accountability for the negligence that destroyed 600 homes.",
    "AB 2070 ensures critical information is available to address risks as they happen.",
    "When fire districts know in advance about risky work being conducted, they can be prepared with personnel ready.",
    "Without prior information from utilities, fire districts can be caught off guard and lose precious moments to stop the flames."
  ]
}
```
